title: Fruity Syrian Corn Stew
description: A bitter, creamy stew that blends dried white corn with yuzu-cured citrus, aromatic spices, and the traditional cataplana cooking method for a nostalgic Syrian flavor.
instructions: Rinse and soak the dried corn kernels in water overnight, then drain before cooking.
Heat the olive oil in a cataplana over medium-high heat, and add the diced onion, sautéing until translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until fragrant.
Add the diced tomato, cumin, coriander, and thyme, cooking until the tomato softens and releases juices.
Pour in 1 cup of water or low-sodium vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the soaked corn kernels, reduce the heat, cover the cataplana, and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the corn is tender.
Stir in the butter and allow it to melt, creating a creamy texture.
Season the stew with salt, pepper, and the fresh yuzu juice, adjusting the acidity to taste.
Remove the cataplana from heat, let the stew rest for 5 minutes to meld flavors.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh thyme leaves.
